aws.name: WAF deployed on internet-facing resources
aws.expected: WAF web ACLs deployed on all internet-facing ALBs, API Gateways, and CloudFront
aws.remediation: Deploy AWS WAF on all internet-facing Application Load Balancers and API Gateways.

aws.name: VPC Flow Logs enabled for all VPCs
aws.expected: Flow logs enabled for all VPCs capturing ACCEPT and REJECT traffic
aws.remediation: Enable VPC Flow Logs on all VPCs in ACCEPT and REJECT mode.

aws.name: Network Firewall deployed for CUI VPCs
aws.expected: AWS Network Firewall deployed for traffic inspection on CUI VPCs
aws.remediation: Deploy AWS Network Firewall for deep packet inspection on CUI VPCs.

aws.name: GuardDuty monitors network anomalies
aws.expected: GuardDuty enabled with VPC Flow Log monitoring active
aws.remediation: Enable GuardDuty with VPC Flow Logs data source.

azure.name: Azure Firewall or NVA deployed
azure.expected: Azure Firewall deployed with threat intelligence-based filtering enabled
azure.remediation: Deploy Azure Firewall Premium with threat intelligence-based filtering.

azure.name: NSG flow logs enabled
azure.expected: NSG flow logs enabled for all Network Security Groups
azure.remediation: Enable NSG flow logs with Traffic Analytics for all NSGs.

azure.name: Azure WAF deployed
azure.expected: WAF policies deployed on all internet-facing Application Gateways and Front Doors
azure.remediation: Deploy Azure WAF on Application Gateways and Front Door.

gcp.name: Cloud Armor WAF deployed
gcp.expected: Cloud Armor security policies deployed on internet-facing load balancers
gcp.remediation: Deploy Cloud Armor security policies on all internet-facing HTTP(S) load balancers.

gcp.name: VPC Flow Logs enabled
gcp.expected: VPC Flow Logs enabled on all subnets
gcp.remediation: Enable VPC Flow Logs on all subnets with appropriate aggregation interval.

gcp.name: Packet Mirroring or IDS configured
gcp.expected: Packet Mirroring or Cloud IDS configured for network inspection
gcp.remediation: Configure Cloud IDS or Packet Mirroring for network traffic inspection.

aws.name: Defense-in-depth architecture layers present
aws.expected: At least 4 of 5 security layers present: network segmentation, GuardDuty, CloudTrail, KMS, VPC Flow Logs
aws.remediation: Implement multiple security layers: VPC segmentation, threat detection (GuardDuty), audit logging (CloudTrail), encryption (KMS), and network monitoring (VPC Flow Logs).

azure.name: Defense-in-depth architecture layers present
azure.expected: At least 4 of 5 security layers present: NSGs on subnets, Key Vault, activity log alerts, Defender for Cloud, Network Watcher
azure.remediation: Implement multiple security layers: NSGs, Key Vault for secrets, Activity Log alerts, Microsoft Defender for Cloud, and Network Watcher.

gcp.name: Defense-in-depth architecture layers present
gcp.expected: At least 3 of 4 security layers present: firewall rules, KMS keys, log sinks, org policy constraints
gcp.remediation: Implement multiple security layers: VPC firewall rules, Cloud KMS encryption, Cloud Logging sinks, and Organization Policy constraints.

objectives_requiring_documentation.text: architectural designs that promote effective information security are identified.
objectives_requiring_documentation.evidence_needed: Provide documentation showing that architectural designs that promote effective information security are identified and documented.

objectives_requiring_documentation.text: software development techniques that promote effective information security are identified.
objectives_requiring_documentation.evidence_needed: Provide documentation showing that software development techniques that promote effective information security are identified and documented.

objectives_requiring_documentation.text: systems engineering principles that promote effective information security are identified.
objectives_requiring_documentation.evidence_needed: Provide documentation showing that systems engineering principles that promote effective information security are identified and documented.

objectives_requiring_documentation.text: identified architectural designs that promote effective information security are employed.
objectives_requiring_documentation.evidence_needed: Provide documentation or process evidence: identified architectural designs that promote effective information security are employed.

objectives_requiring_documentation.text: identified software development techniques that promote effective information security are employed.
objectives_requiring_documentation.evidence_needed: Provide documentation or process evidence: identified software development techniques that promote effective information security are employed.

objectives_requiring_documentation.text: identified systems engineering principles that promote effective information security are employed.
objectives_requiring_documentation.evidence_needed: Provide documentation or process evidence: identified systems engineering principles that promote effective information security are employed.

aws.name: Management and data plane separated
aws.expected: Management subnets are isolated from data/application subnets
aws.remediation: Separate management interfaces into dedicated subnets with restricted access.

aws.name: Systems Manager for management access
aws.expected: SSM Session Manager used for management instead of direct SSH/RDP
aws.remediation: Use SSM Session Manager for server management instead of direct SSH/RDP access.

aws.name: Client VPN full tunnel enforced
aws.expected: Split tunnel disabled on Client VPN endpoints (SplitTunnel=false)
aws.remediation: Disable split tunneling on AWS Client VPN endpoints.

azure.name: Management network isolated
azure.expected: Dedicated management VNet or subnet with restricted NSG rules
azure.remediation: Create dedicated management subnet/VNet with restrictive NSG rules.

azure.name: VPN forced tunneling configured
azure.expected: Forced tunneling enabled to prevent split tunnel scenarios
azure.remediation: Enable forced tunneling on VPN gateway connections.

gcp.name: Management network segmented
gcp.expected: Dedicated management subnet with restricted firewall rules
gcp.remediation: Create dedicated management subnet with restrictive firewall rules.

gcp.name: VPN full tunnel policy enforced
gcp.expected: VPN configuration routes all traffic through tunnel (no split tunnel)
gcp.remediation: Configure VPN to route all traffic through tunnel, disabling split tunneling.

aws.name: EBS volumes not shared across accounts
aws.expected: EBS snapshots not shared publicly or with unauthorized accounts
aws.remediation: Remove public sharing from EBS snapshots and restrict to authorized accounts.

aws.name: AMIs not publicly shared
aws.expected: No AMIs shared publicly
aws.remediation: Remove public sharing from AMIs.

azure.name: Shared disks restricted
azure.expected: Shared disk feature limited to authorized multi-attach scenarios only
azure.remediation: Restrict shared disk usage to explicitly approved scenarios.

gcp.name: Images not publicly shared
gcp.expected: No compute images shared with allUsers or allAuthenticatedUsers
gcp.remediation: Remove public IAM bindings from custom compute images.

aws.name: Public subnets isolated from private subnets
aws.expected: Public subnets (with IGW route) are separate from private subnets
aws.remediation: Ensure public-facing resources are in dedicated public subnets separated from private.

aws.name: NAT Gateway used for private subnet outbound
aws.expected: Private subnets use NAT Gateway for outbound internet access
aws.remediation: Deploy NAT Gateway for private subnet outbound connectivity.

azure.name: DMZ subnet implemented
azure.expected: Dedicated DMZ/perimeter subnet for public-facing workloads
azure.remediation: Create dedicated DMZ subnet for public-facing resources with NSG isolation.

gcp.name: Public-facing resources in dedicated subnets
gcp.expected: Public-facing workloads isolated in dedicated subnets with restricted firewall rules
gcp.remediation: Isolate public-facing resources in dedicated subnets with specific firewall rules.

aws.name: Default security group restricts all traffic
aws.expected: Default security groups have no inbound or outbound rules (deny all)
aws.remediation: Remove all rules from default security groups in every VPC.

aws.name: NACLs implement deny-by-default
aws.expected: Network ACLs have explicit deny rules and allow only necessary traffic
aws.remediation: Configure NACLs with explicit allow rules and deny-all default.

azure.name: NSG default deny rules active
azure.expected: All NSGs have DenyAllInBound as the last rule (default behavior)
azure.remediation: Verify NSG default deny rules are not overridden by overly permissive allow rules.

azure.name: Azure Firewall default deny configured
azure.expected: Azure Firewall configured with deny-by-default and explicit allow rules
azure.remediation: Configure Azure Firewall with deny-all default and explicit allow rules.

gcp.name: Default deny ingress firewall rule
gcp.expected: Implied deny-all ingress rule active with explicit allow rules only for required traffic
gcp.remediation: GCP has implied deny-all ingress; verify no overly permissive rules override it.

gcp.name: Default allow egress reviewed
gcp.expected: Default allow-all egress rule replaced with specific egress rules
gcp.remediation: Replace default allow-all egress with specific egress firewall rules.

aws.name: ALB/NLB listeners use TLS 1.2+
aws.expected: All HTTPS listeners use TLS 1.2+ security policies
aws.remediation: Update load balancer listeners to ELBSecurityPolicy-TLS13-1-2-2021-06 or newer.

aws.name: CloudFront uses TLS 1.2+
aws.expected: All CloudFront distributions enforce TLS 1.2 minimum
aws.remediation: Set CloudFront minimum protocol version to TLSv1.2_2021.

aws.name: S3 bucket policy enforces TLS
aws.expected: S3 bucket policies deny requests without aws:SecureTransport
aws.remediation: Add bucket policy condition to deny requests where aws:SecureTransport is false.

azure.name: Minimum TLS 1.2 enforced on App Services
azure.expected: MinTlsVersion=1.2 on all App Service web apps
azure.remediation: Set minimum TLS version to 1.2 on all Azure App Service web apps.

azure.name: Storage accounts enforce TLS 1.2+
azure.expected: MinimumTlsVersion=TLS1_2 on all storage accounts
azure.remediation: Set minimum TLS version to 1.2 on all storage accounts.

gcp.name: SSL policies enforce TLS 1.2+
gcp.expected: All SSL policies set minTlsVersion to TLS_1_2
gcp.remediation: Configure SSL policies with minimum TLS 1.2 on all load balancers.

gcp.name: Cloud SQL requires SSL
gcp.expected: requireSsl=true on all Cloud SQL instances
gcp.remediation: Enable SSL enforcement on all Cloud SQL instances.

aws.name: ALB idle timeout configured
aws.expected: Idle timeout set to 60 seconds or less on ALBs
aws.remediation: Configure ALB idle timeout to 60 seconds or appropriate value.

aws.name: API Gateway timeout configured
aws.expected: API Gateway integration timeout appropriately configured
aws.remediation: Configure API Gateway timeout settings for session termination.

azure.name: Application Gateway idle timeout configured
azure.expected: Connection idle timeout configured on Application Gateways
azure.remediation: Configure idle timeout on Application Gateway frontend IP configurations.

gcp.name: Load balancer timeout configured
gcp.expected: Backend service timeout configured appropriately
gcp.remediation: Configure backend service timeout on load balancers.

aws.name: KMS key rotation enabled
aws.expected: Automatic key rotation enabled on all customer-managed KMS keys
aws.remediation: Enable automatic annual key rotation on all customer-managed KMS keys.

aws.name: KMS key policies restrict access
aws.expected: KMS key policies follow least privilege with no wildcard principals
aws.remediation: Review KMS key policies and restrict to authorized principals.

aws.name: ACM certificates managed and auto-renewed
aws.expected: All TLS certificates managed through ACM with auto-renewal
aws.remediation: Use ACM-managed certificates with automatic renewal.

azure.name: Key Vault key rotation configured
azure.expected: Key rotation policies configured on all encryption keys
azure.remediation: Configure key rotation policies in Azure Key Vault.

azure.name: Key Vault access policies follow least privilege
azure.expected: Key Vault access policies restrict key operations to authorized identities
azure.remediation: Review and restrict Key Vault access policies to authorized identities.

gcp.name: Cloud KMS key rotation configured
gcp.expected: Automatic rotation period configured on all encryption keys
gcp.remediation: Configure automatic key rotation on Cloud KMS keys.

gcp.name: Cloud KMS IAM bindings restricted
gcp.expected: KMS key ring and key IAM bindings follow least privilege
gcp.remediation: Restrict Cloud KMS IAM bindings to authorized service accounts and users.

aws.name: FIPS endpoints used in GovCloud
aws.expected: Applications use FIPS 140-2 validated endpoints in GovCloud regions
aws.remediation: Configure SDK/CLI to use FIPS endpoints (e.g., iam-fips.us-gov-west-1.amazonaws.com).

aws.name: S3 uses FIPS-validated encryption
aws.expected: S3 bucket encryption uses SSE-KMS with FIPS-validated KMS
aws.remediation: Use SSE-KMS encryption which leverages FIPS 140-2 validated HSMs.

azure.name: Azure Government FIPS-validated services used
azure.expected: VMs in Government regions use FIPS-enabled OS configurations
azure.remediation: Enable FIPS mode on Windows VMs or use FIPS-validated Linux configurations.

gcp.name: CMEK uses FIPS-validated Cloud KMS
gcp.expected: CMEK keys use Cloud KMS which provides FIPS 140-2 Level 3 HSM backing
gcp.remediation: Use Cloud KMS or Cloud HSM for FIPS-validated cryptographic operations.

SC-15.manual_guidance: Review policies for collaborative computing devices (cameras, microphones, smart displays, conferencing systems). Verify remote activation is disabled by default, physical disconnect options are available, and active indicators (LEDs, on-screen notifications) inform users when devices are in use.

objectives_requiring_documentation.evidence_needed: Provide documentation showing that collaborative computing devices are identified and documented.

objectives_requiring_documentation.text: collaborative computing devices provide indication to users of devices in use.
objectives_requiring_documentation.evidence_needed: Provide documentation or process evidence: collaborative computing devices provide indication to users of devices in use.

objectives_requiring_documentation.text: remote activation of collaborative computing devices is prohibited.
objectives_requiring_documentation.evidence_needed: Provide documentation or process evidence: remote activation of collaborative computing devices is prohibited.

aws.name: WAF rules block malicious scripts
aws.expected: WAF managed rule groups include XSS and SQL injection protection
aws.remediation: Enable AWS Managed Rules for common threats including XSS protection.

azure.name: WAF OWASP rules enabled
azure.expected: WAF policies include OWASP managed rule sets with XSS/injection rules
azure.remediation: Enable OWASP managed rule set on Azure WAF policies.

gcp.name: Cloud Armor preconfigured WAF rules
gcp.expected: Cloud Armor policies include preconfigured rules for XSS and code injection
gcp.remediation: Enable Cloud Armor preconfigured WAF rules for XSS and injection attacks.

SC-28.manual_guidance: Review VoIP security policies and configurations. Verify VoIP traffic is encrypted (SRTP/TLS), segregated on dedicated VLANs, and monitored for anomalous activity. Check that VoIP systems used for CUI discussions are assessed for eavesdropping risks.

objectives_requiring_documentation.text: use of Voice over Internet Protocol (VoIP) technologies is controlled.
objectives_requiring_documentation.evidence_needed: Provide documentation or process evidence: use of Voice over Internet Protocol (VoIP) technologies is controlled.

objectives_requiring_documentation.text: use of Voice over Internet Protocol (VoIP) technologies is monitored.
objectives_requiring_documentation.evidence_needed: Provide documentation or process evidence: use of Voice over Internet Protocol (VoIP) technologies is monitored.

aws.name: ACM certificates valid and not expired
aws.expected: No expired or soon-to-expire certificates (within 30 days)
aws.remediation: Renew or replace expiring certificates; use ACM auto-renewal where possible.

aws.name: DNSSEC enabled on Route 53 hosted zones
aws.expected: DNSSEC signing enabled on public hosted zones
aws.remediation: Enable DNSSEC signing on public Route 53 hosted zones.

azure.name: App Service certificates valid
azure.expected: No expired or expiring TLS certificates on App Service
azure.remediation: Renew expiring certificates or use App Service managed certificates.

gcp.name: Managed SSL certificates valid
gcp.expected: All SSL certificates are valid and not expired
gcp.remediation: Use Google-managed SSL certificates for automatic renewal.

aws.name: S3 default encryption enabled
aws.expected: Default encryption (SSE-S3 or SSE-KMS) enabled on all buckets
aws.remediation: Enable default encryption with SSE-KMS on all S3 buckets.

aws.name: RDS encryption at rest enabled
aws.expected: StorageEncrypted=true on all RDS instances
aws.remediation: Enable encryption at rest on all RDS instances.

aws.name: DynamoDB encryption enabled
aws.expected: SSESpecification enabled with KMS key on all DynamoDB tables
aws.remediation: Enable encryption with customer-managed KMS key on DynamoDB tables.

aws.name: EBS encryption by default enabled
aws.expected: EBS encryption by default enabled in all regions
aws.remediation: Enable EBS encryption by default in all active regions.

azure.name: Storage account encryption with CMK
azure.expected: Storage accounts use customer-managed keys for encryption
azure.remediation: Configure storage accounts to use customer-managed keys from Key Vault.

azure.name: Azure SQL TDE with CMK
azure.expected: TDE enabled with customer-managed key on all SQL databases
azure.remediation: Configure TDE with customer-managed key from Key Vault.

azure.name: VM disk encryption enabled
azure.expected: All VM disks use Azure Disk Encryption or server-side encryption with CMK
azure.remediation: Enable disk encryption on all VMs using ADE or SSE with CMK.

gcp.name: Cloud Storage CMEK encryption
gcp.expected: All buckets with CUI data use customer-managed encryption keys
gcp.remediation: Configure Cloud Storage buckets with CMEK from Cloud KMS.

gcp.name: Cloud SQL CMEK encryption
gcp.expected: All Cloud SQL instances use customer-managed encryption keys
gcp.remediation: Create Cloud SQL instances with CMEK encryption.

gcp.name: BigQuery CMEK encryption
gcp.expected: BigQuery datasets use customer-managed encryption keys
gcp.remediation: Configure BigQuery datasets with CMEK from Cloud KMS.
